frameworks.name: program induction
frameworks.one_line: Cultural knowledge represented as programs learned from examples.

frameworks.name: Bayesian program learning
frameworks.one_line: Inferring latent programs from observed input-output data.

core_question: Does observing the execution trace of a cultural process, not just its input and output, accelerate program induction and thereby support high-fidelity social learning?
key_assumptions: Cultural processes can be represented as programs
Social learners observe causal steps, not only end products
manipulations: Learning from input-output pairs vs. learning from full execution traces
Number of training examples (down to a single example)
key_findings: Observing an execution trace speeds up program induction relative to seeing only inputs and outputs
Trace-based learning succeeds even from a single example
interpretations: Execution-trace observation can explain the high-fidelity social learning underlying cumulative human culture
The same mechanism may unify emulation and imitation and account for ritualization in moral learning
